description_long: This windmill features a rustic wooden lattice tower constructed from thick, uneven logs, reinforced with horizontal and diagonal braces for stability. At the top, a set of five broad, flat metal blades are mounted on a central hub, forming a classic windmill rotor. The blades are attached to a dark, boxy nacelle that houses the windmill's mechanical components. The structure is weathered, with the wood showing natural grain and the metal blades displaying a matte, slightly aged finish. A thin rod or tail extends downward from the nacelle, likely for stabilization or orientation. The overall design is utilitarian, evoking traditional rural windmills but suitable for decorative or functional use in urban parks or gardens.
description: A rustic wooden lattice windmill with five broad metal blades and a dark nacelle, featuring weathered wood and matte metal surfaces.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all five metal blades and the full wooden lattice tower structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden legs and the depth of the nacelle behind the blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the nacelle and the supporting wooden framework, with blades partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the profile of the blades and the angular wooden supports.
